rulings.Case Title: PARK TERRACE AT MISSION BAY OWNERS ASSOCIATION VS. 5TH & BERRY LLC, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Third Amended Complt
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, October 4, 2013, Line 1, PLAINTIFF PARK TERRACE AT MISSION BAY OWNERS ASSOCIATION'S, MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT Plaintiff's motion for leave to file a Third Amended Complaint is granted. The motion was filed shortly after the stay on discovery was lifted and after the deposition of Defendant EFCO Corporation's Person Most Knowledgeable was taken on August 7, 2013. This order is without prejudice to a motion to continue the trial. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: EQUABLE ASCENT FINANCIAL, LLC VS. BENITO TAYLOR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, October 4, 2013, Line 3, DEFENDANT BENITO TAYLOR'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND The Motion to Set Aside Entry of Default and Default Judgment is granted. Defendant is entitled to relief under Cal. Civ. Proc. § 473.5 because he lacked actual notice of the action in time to defend. Defendant may file his proposed answer within 10 days. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: RAMON PADILLA VS. TOYOTA MOTOR SALES USA INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Andm Otion For Payment Of Attorneys Fees And Reimbursment Of Expensers
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, October 4, 2013, Line 4, PLAINTIFF RAMON PADILLA'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Payment Of Attorneys Fees And Reimbursment Of Expenses The Court has carefully reviewed each challenged time entry and made reductions totaling $3,726.50 for time the Court concluded was unnecessary, insufficiently supported, or not properly compensated. The Court declines to award a multiplier in light of the billing rate requested that adequately compensates for uncertainty and delay factors inherent in this kind of litigation. The Court otherwise determines that the billing rate and time spent are reasonable under the circumstances of this case, and the requested costs and expenses are properly ordered reimbursed. The total fees and costs to be paid by Defendant to Plaintiff are $49,204.50. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: JUAN CHAVARRIA et al VS. BAYVIEW LOAN SERVICING, A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, October 4, 2013, Line 10, DEFENDANT BAYVIEW LOAN SERVICING, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT The motion for summary judgment is denied and the Court denies summary adjudication on the breach of contract cause of action. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether defendant breached paragraphs 2 and 3 of the Deed of Trust by allowing the State Farm policy to lapse and/or be cancelled without notice to plaintiff. The motion for summary adjudication is granted as to the negligence cause of action. Cal. Ins. Code § 673 does not apply to this case. Defendant's duty to plaintiff arises from contract and there is no independent tort duty that defendant breached. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: TERRY MCCOY et al VS. BALWANTSINH THAKOR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Enforce Subpoena Over Written Objections (City College)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 4, 2013, Line 1: Defendants BALWANTSINH THAKOR'S, LATABEN THAKOR'S, and SHREE JALARAM HOTEL LP'S Motion To Enforce Subpoena Over Written Objections (City College) Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendants B.T.L. THAKOR, et al's Motion to Enforce Subpoena Over Written Objections is GRANTED. Plaintiffs failed to file a Motion to Quash under C.C.P. § 1987.1. The parties are required to appear and advise the Court as to the status of a stipulation between the parties concerning the scope of the subpoenas at issue. Defendants' request for sanctions is DENIED. Moving party to submit a proposed Order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SHELLEY KRAMER VS. KAUFMAN DOLOWICH VOLUCK & GONZO LLP, A LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Protective Order Re: Documents Produced By Third Parties Pursuant To Subpoena, Including 4 Medical Providers And One Post Termination Employer And For Sanctions Against Pltf'S Counsel
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 4, 2013, Line 4: Defendants KATHERINE CATLOS' and KAUFMAN DOLOWICH VOLUCK GONZO, LLP'S Motion For Protective Order Re: Documents Produced By Third Parties Pursuant To Subpoena, Including 4 Medical Providers And One Post Termination Employer And For Sanctions Against Plaintiff's Counsel Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendants KAUFMAN DOLOWICH VOLUCK, LLP and KATHERINE CATLOS' Motion for Protective Order re Documents Produced by Third Parties Pursuant to Subpoena, Etc. is GRANTED. Each party is ordered to bring its version of a Stipulated Protective Order to the hearing. Parties are ordered to meet and confer and, after hearing on the matter, submit a joint Stipulated Protective Order to the Court. Defendants' request for sanction is DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
